$CRDO is a ~5% position in my growth portfolio. 
$ALAB is a ~4% position in my family portfolio.

The $AMZN & Anthropic deal is especially bullish for $ALAB & $CRDO because 5 GW of committed Trainium capacity means years of AI cluster buildout at AWS & both companies live inside that stack.

Astera handles the rack-scale connectivity & memory layer helping data move reliably across chips, memory & fabric as clusters get larger & faster across generations.

Credo built its business on the physical interconnect layer inside AI racks so if AWS is already ~86% of Credo's revenue then a decade of contractually committed Trainium buildout is about as locked-in as demand gets in hardware.

Both names are bets on the layer of the AI stack that actually has to move the data once the compute gets deployed.

Is the SaaSpocalypse now over?

📺 POWER PLAYS ARE BACK – HERE ARE SOME SETUPS

Power Plays are back as the market shifts from a cautious, corrective phase to a momentum-driven environment where buying dips is working again. 

Get Stock Ideas: t3live.com/power 
Get Options Ideas: t3live.com/ppo

Traders are getting more aggressive, but execution matters: scale in, sell into strength, and reload on pullbacks. 

– $AMZN is coiling near a potential breakout but needs to clear and hold resistance.
– $AAPL already delivered a strong move and may consolidate before the next leg.
– $RKLB showed relative strength vs peers $ASTS and became tradable at the right time. It previously failed due to a dilution event, but then it set up again.

So, don't just pick stocks; timing, discipline, and actively managing positions as conditions improve are what really matter.

📺 BULLISH BUT EXTENDED – TRIM INTO STRENGTH

Trade LIVE with me this week for free: 

🛑 secure.t3live.com/alpha-team…

A key test from the prior session (whether $SPY would hold Friday’s low) was passed, giving traders confidence to adjust their positions.

This allowed those who were overly hedged or short (including me) to reduce downside exposure, cover shorts, and gradually get “back onside” with the market.

The market is still trending higher, but indicators show some extension:
– The oscillator has cooled slightly (from ~+80 to ~+60), meaning momentum is still strong but less overheated.
– The 8-day remains below price, reinforcing that the market is stretched in the short term.

Because of this setup, here is my approach:
– Trim into strength (take partial profits as the market moves higher instead of chasing);
– Stay flexible (lock in gains so you’re ready to buy dips rather than getting trapped at highs);
– Avoid overexposure (especially important if you were previously “offsides” (wrongly positioned), as many traders were).

*

The key level to watch today is $712.38 on $SPY. 

If price breaks and holds above this level, it could open the door to further highs and continuation of the trend.

If not, some consolidation or pullback could follow, making dip-buying opportunities more relevant.

*

Sometimes the best move is to step aside rather than force trades in uncertain conditions.

Always trim profits into strength.

I fast regularly. Not because it's easy — because it's training.

Missing a meal when everyone's eating teaches you something markets can't: sitting with discomfort is a skill.

When $SPY hits all-time highs and euphoria peaks, the investor who trained for discomfort wins.

$BTC at $77,501 — riding the risk-on wave.

Up from $73.5K earlier in the week.

But here's what doesn't change with price: Bitcoin mining costs hover near $80K.

Miner stress below cost-of-production is a structural overhang the bulls haven't fully priced in.

At the March 30 low, every major U.S. index was RED for 2026.

$SPY. $QQQ. $IWM. $DIA. All negative.

Four weeks later: all at ALL-TIME HIGHS.

The full reversal from across-the-board red to across-the-board records is historically rare. Don't dismiss it.

$TSLA reports earnings April 22.

That same week: 94 $SPY component companies report.

The busiest earnings week of the year hits when markets are at ALL-TIME HIGHS.

Earnings either validate this rally — or give every reluctant bear a reason to act.

$SLV surged 6.5% in a week to $80.78 — outperforming $GLD.

Silver has BOTH the monetary metal bid AND industrial demand tailwinds.

When silver outperforms gold this aggressively, it's usually telling you something about where the economy is heading.

$GLD at $4,868 per oz — holding near record highs.

Equities at all-time highs. Oil crashing. Risk-on everywhere.

And gold is OUTPERFORMING every major index YTD.

When safe-haven demand persists through a risk-on melt-up, that's a signal worth watching.
